history_headlines.headline: The statements of the IDF spokesperson: "This is a blatant violation of the ceasefire agreement, and the IDF views this with great severity" repeating again and again, after the serious violations and attempts to harm our forces, are starting to look like the statements after rocket fire from the Gaza Strip to southern cities in the years preceding the Hamas massacre "will pay a heavy price". We saw how Hamas paid a heavy price over the years. If it had paid a heavy and real price the massacre would not have occurred. These words have no meaning as long as the enemy continues to violate the agreement every few hours/days, and our response is a few pinpoint strikes or an interception here and there. The enemy is testing us, trying to harm our forces, rehabilitating and strengthening while we continue to uphold the agreement. This is exactly what the return to 10/6 looks like. If these are our responses to violations, no wonder Hamas continues to violate the agreement - it simply pays no price for these violations.
history_headlines.keywords: IDF spokesperson, statements, violation, ceasefire agreement, severity, forces, enemy, testing, harm, response, strikes, interception, Hamas, violations

history_headlines.headline: One of the matters that is important to pay attention to in this story tonight - is the fact that the terrorists came out of a tunnel shaft. Beit Hanoun is a town where the IDF has been operating continuously for more than two years, division after division has entered to operate in this area, and to destroy what is found there above and below ground. And even after more than two years - it turns out that not all tunnels have been exposed, and there are still active Hamas tunnels there that the IDF did not know about - and the terrorist cell came out of one of them. This teaches about the enormous scope of tunnels that remain, even east of the yellow line, tunnels that are located near our forces and could be used by Hamas terrorists to attack the IDF's forward positions. During a visit to the yellow line areas this week, in the northern Gaza Strip, commanders said that only a small part of the tunnels in the area have been destroyed - and there is still a lot of work to be done. Pay attention to this documentation we are publishing now: This is what a Hamas tunnel looks like that was photographed in recent days, within the yellow line areas under Israeli control - and this tunnel passes beneath an IDF forward position in the Jabalia area. The IDF stated that the tunnel is known and our forces are working to destroy it. More evidence of the endless quantity of tunnels that still exist in the yellow line areas - and also pass beneath the permanent IDF positions in the area. Doron Kadosh
history_headlines.keywords: tunnels, Hamas, IDF, terrorists, attack, forward positions, Gaza Strip, yellow line, Jabalia, destruction, documentation, commanders, operations

history_headlines.headline: Transfer of YLC-8B radar from China to Iran could change its air power in the Middle East and put an end to stealth dominance. The YLC-8B is a long-range UHF frequency three-dimensional stealth tracking radar that uses full coherent digital pulse compression and dynamic target identification technology. This radar is adapted for early warning detection of airborne targets and also has sufficient resolution to perform target tracking functions. According to reports, the YLC-8B radar has the ability to track stealth aircraft thanks to its low operating frequency in the UHF band. Evolving intelligence assessments that China has supplied several YLC-8B radar systems - each capable of detecting targets up to 700 km away - signal a strategic recalibration in Middle East air defense architecture, which directly challenges long-standing assumptions underlying US and Israeli stealth attack doctrines in Iranian territory. For the United States, the radar's reported ability to detect B-2 Spirit bombers at long ranges complicates contingency planning for deep strike missions against hardened Iranian facilities, which may require different force packages. Gili R. Security 301 Arab World on Telegram Http://t.me/arabworld301news
history_headlines.keywords: radar, China, Iran, air power, Middle East, stealth, detection, tracking, US, Israel, bombers, defense, systems, technology, military

history_headlines.headline: Situation picture - February 15 System 301 editor - Dr. Doron Matza 1. Negotiations between the US and Iran will resume this week. 2. The impression is that under the circumstances that have been created, if an American attack on Iran occurs, it will converge to a very specific model. 3. The time that has passed since Trump promised assistance to the massacred masses in Iran, Iran's preparation for an attack, the long duration of US force deployment, and mainly the strategy that Iran admits publicly it will adopt in case of an American attack, neutralize the possibility of a measured, short, and limited American move like Midnight Hammer or the Venezuela model. 4. The systemic concept that Iran presents versus the American one is based on a chaotic response. Meaning, a broad response throughout the Middle East, against the American and Israeli adversary, in broad geographical foci, including those of the Gulf states, and perhaps even beyond the local neighborhood towards Europe. Against the American idea of an aggressive and one-time blow, Iran will present its own idea based on the opposite logic of a long, ongoing, multi-theater, and multi-focal campaign. 5. In fact, the Iranian idea is based more or less on the one adopted by Hamas throughout the last and unfinished war in Gaza. The idea is to create ongoing chaos and disruption that will deter the US from continuing the campaign and lead to its end under conditions different from those Trump dreamed of. 6. Therefore, the US will probably have no choice but to turn what was originally planned to be measured and precise into a campaign with one purpose: toppling the regime, which is the only alternative through which the US can cut off the Iranian chaos strategy and the effort to turn the second Iran-US-Israel war into another type of campaign like Gaza or Russia-Ukraine. 7. In itself, this is not a bad scenario from Israel's perspective. The opposite is true. All this of course provided that the Iranians do not fold in the negotiations and do what they did in their war against Iraq [surrender that became a strategic growth lever] and provided that Trump is not ready to adopt partial arrangements tending to permanence that also have imaginary logic, reminiscent of his dot plan in the Gaza Strip. For publication in 301: Yossi Eliezer 301 The Arab World on Telegram
history_headlines.keywords: negotiations, US, Iran, attack, American attack, force deployment, strategy, chaotic response, Middle East, multi-theater campaign, regime toppling, Trump, Netanyahu, Gaza, Russia-Ukraine

history_headlines.headline: Saudi Foreign Ministry: The Saudi Foreign Minister met yesterday on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference with Rabbi Yehuda Kaploun, the special envoy of the Trump administration to combat anti-Semitism. Rabbi Kaploun was born in Kfar Chabad in Israel. Both his parents were emissaries of the Lubavitcher Rebbe in Kfar Chabad. He is a businessman, philanthropist and a great lover of Israel. ## It is interesting to see that the Saudi Foreign Ministry chose to announce the meeting officially and publicly. I will mention that recently I published current documentation of the writing of a Torah scroll in the capital of Saudi Arabia. [To respond to the article click here](
history_headlines.keywords: Saudi, Foreign Ministry, meeting, Munich Security Conference, Rabbi, anti-Semitism, Israel, Torah

history_headlines.headline: The American New York Post: The American envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner reported to Trump that it is difficult to almost impossible to reach an agreement with Iran on the nuclear issue.
history_headlines.keywords: New York Post, envoys, Trump, agreement, Iran, nuclear

history_headlines.headline: A complex week ahead of us... It started already at 0330 in the morning, Iranian naval maneuvers began in the southeastern part of Iran and in the south and southwest, in simple words, the most dangerous area in Iran is the eastern entrance area of the Strait of Hormuz, this is the soft underbelly of Iran because only there it is possible to land forces from the air or sea. This area is saturated with coastal missile systems, any ship that comes within range will sink within a minute. Starting tomorrow, Iran will turn off the radar operating in the Isfahan area. In fact, all these moves that Iran is making have one reason, to create a kind of shield that blocks any American possibility to attack or land special forces in different areas in western Iran. The exercises are actually the cover for real moves that Iran is taking. At the same time, on Tuesday, another meeting is supposed to take place in Switzerland between the USA and Iran, a meeting that is destined for failure from the start, why? Because the USA passed in recent days its conditions for continuing the meetings, and they were rejected outright by Iran. So surely you are going crazy if everything is known in advance why do they still meet? The answer is incredibly simple, because when talking, they don't fight🤪 because when talking both sides continue to prepare for the military move in short both sides are buying time. Abu Saleh's Desk Arabic
history_headlines.keywords: Iran, naval maneuvers, Strait of Hormuz, coastal missile systems, radar, Isfahan, shield, American attack, special forces, western Iran, exercises, USA, meeting, Switzerland, military move

history_headlines.headline: Ismaili became the head of Dafoos University of the Army by order of the Commander-in-Chief of the Army, Brigadier General 'Farzad Ismaili' was appointed as the new head of the Army Command and Staff University. Amir Ismaili is an experienced and seasoned commander in the field of air defense and previously held responsibilities including command of the Khatam al-Anbiya Air Defense Base. Cyber Guards Corps @SEPAH
history_headlines.keywords: Ismaili, head, Dafoos University, Army, Commander-in-Chief, Brigadier General, Farzad Ismaili, Command and Staff University, air defense, Khatam al-Anbiya, Cyber Guards Corps

joke: Chris Rock: So Obama says there are aliens out there, but they’re not in Area 51—man, I think we all know where they really are! They’re not hidden, they’re just sitting at the kitchen table of some conspiracy theorist, sipping on their coffee, like, “Yeah, sure, I look like a green man living on Mars, but the real crazy is the guy down the street who thinks the airport in the Jezreel Valley is gonna fix the traffic! Sorry, buddy, aliens or not, you better get used to those delays!” They said compromise is key—if you're waiting for Martians to throw in on your negotiations, you might be waiting a long time!
